summary:
Accessory canals are mostly concentrated in the apical third; however, they also frequently occur in the middle third region as the second-highest concentration site compared to other areas such as the coronal or cervical parts of a tooth. The distribution reflects anatomical factors related to vascular support near these sections within each root segment. Agent2 reasoned based primarily on common knowledge regarding dentistry and endodontics focusing specifically on locations with high density for accessory canals outside just the very tips of roots. Key takeaway was identifying secondary hotspot being mid-root portion over others not specified directly by initial query context provided initially before expanding understanding through standard medical/dental references available typically when addressing questions involving human body structures including oral cavity components.

formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 1 summarized): Accessory canals are mostly concentrated in the apical third; however, they also frequently occur in the middle third region as the second-highest concentration site compared to other areas such as the coronal or cervical parts of a tooth. The distribution reflects anatomical factors related to vascular support near these sections within each root segment. Agent2 reasoned based primarily on common knowledge regarding dentistry and endodontics focusing specifically on locations with high density for accessory canals outside just the very tips of roots. Key takeaway was identifying secondary hotspot being mid-root portion over others not specified directly by initial query context provided initially before expanding understanding through standard medical/dental references available typically when addressing questions involving human body structures including oral cavity components.
